vcpu_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 anchors m6i.large 2v/8GB=$0.102, r6i.large 2v/16GB=$0.1292 → g=$0.0034, v=$0.0374 (SG m6a/r6a ให้ v=$0.0396)

ram_gb_hour.notes: จาก anchor เดียวกับ vcpu_hour

block_gb_mo.service: Amazon EBS General Purpose SSD (gp3) storage
block_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7. Includes free baseline 3,000 IOPS and 125 MB/s per volume; extra IOPS $0.0054/provisioned IOPS-mo, extra throughput $0.0432/MBps-mo. Verified via AWS Price List API offer file (usagetype APS9-EBS:VolumeUsage.gp3).

os_std_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7, first-50TB/month tier ($0.0216 next 450TB, $0.0207 over 500TB). Verified via AWS Price List API.

os_ia_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7. Min 30-day/128KB charges apply. Verified via AWS Price List API.

os_arch_gb_mo.service: Amazon S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval storage
os_arch_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7. Min 90-day charge; retrieval fees extra. Verified via AWS Price List API (APS9-TimedStorage-GlacierByteHrs).

os_retrieval_gb.notes: ap-southeast-7, flat fee for all bytes retrieved from Standard-IA (APS9-Retrieval-SIA).

os_req_10k.service: Amazon S3 GET and all other (Tier 2) requests
os_req_10k.notes: ap-southeast-7. GET-based: $0.0036 per 10,000 GET/SELECT and all other requests ($0.00000036/request). PUT/COPY/POST/LIST (Tier 1) cost more. Verified via AWS Price List API.

fss_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7 Standard (frequent-access) class; EFS Infrequent Access $0.0245, Archive $0.009 per GB-mo. Verified via AWS Price List API.

alb_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 (APS9-LoadBalancerUsage, Load Balancer-Application). Verified via AWS Price List API.

lcu_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. LCU = max of 25 new conn/s, 3,000 active conn/min, 1 GB/hr processed (EC2 targets), 1,000 rule evaluations/s.

nlb_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. NLB capacity unit (NLCU) additionally $0.006/NLCU-hr. Verified via AWS Price List API.

fw_endpoint_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 primary endpoint ($0.264/hr for secondary/HA endpoint). Verified via AWS Price List API.

fw_gb.notes: ap-southeast-7 (APS9-Traffic-GB-Processed). NAT Gateway processing discount applies when combined.

waf_acl_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7, prorated hourly. PLUS $1.00 per rule per month per web ACL (APS9-RuleV2); managed rule groups count. Verified via AWS Price List API price fields.

waf_1m_req.notes: ap-southeast-7 base tier (Tier0/up-to-1500-WCU); higher WCU tiers cost $0.80-$2.00 per 1M. Remember the per-rule monthly fee noted in waf_acl_mo.

dx_1g_hour.notes: Port at Telehouse Bangkok (THRA9) homed to ap-southeast-7 (APS9-THRA9-PortUsage:1G). Hosted-connection 1G is $0.33/hr. Data transfer out over DX billed separately.

vpn_conn_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 (APS9-VPN-Usage-Hours:ipsec.1). Transit Gateway VPN attachment adds $0.07/hr + $0.02/GB processed.

egress_gb.service: Data Transfer Out to Internet from ap-southeast-7
egress_gb.notes: FIRST TIER (0-10TB/month) = $0.108/GB, after 100GB/month global free tier. Next 40TB $0.0765, next 100TB (to 150TB) $0.0738, over 150TB $0.072. Verified via AWS Price List API (AWSDataTransfer, APS9-DataTransfer-Out-Bytes).

pg_vcpu_hour.service: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL Single-AZ (derived from db.m6i.large / db.r6i.large)

pg_ram_gb_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. Same solve as pg_vcpu_hour. Check: 2*0.0825+16*0.005625=$0.255 = db.r6i.large.

pg_storage_gb_mo.notes: ap-southeast-7, same $0.124 across engines Single-AZ (APS9-RDS:GP3-Storage). Baseline 3,000 IOPS/125MBs included (<400GB volumes).

mysql_vcpu_hour.service: Amazon RDS for MySQL Single-AZ (derived from db.m6i.large / db.r6i.large)
mysql_vcpu_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. MySQL instance prices identical to PostgreSQL: db.m6i.large $0.210, db.r6i.large $0.255 (verified). Same solve -> v=$0.0825, g=$0.005625.

oracle_se2_li_vcpu_hour.service: Amazon RDS for Oracle SE2 License-Included Single-AZ (derived from db.m7i.large / db.r7i.large)
oracle_se2_li_vcpu_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. db.m5/db.r5 are NOT offered in Thailand; used current-gen db.m7i.large 2vCPU/8GB=$0.46/hr and db.r7i.large 2vCPU/16GB=$0.504/hr (verified, SE2 LI Single-AZ). Solve: g=(0.504-0.46)/8=$0.0055/GB-hr, v=(0.46-8*0.0055)/2=$0.208/vCPU-hr. Oracle EE …

k8s_cluster_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. Extended-support versions bill $0.50/cluster-hr. Worker nodes/Fargate billed separately.

redis_gb_hour.service: Amazon ElastiCache Serverless (Redis OSS) data stored
redis_gb_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7. Valkey engine is cheaper: $0.085/GB-hr. Plus compute: $0.0035 per million ECPUs (Redis; Valkey $0.0023/M). Min 100MB (Valkey) / 1GB (Redis) metered.

lambda_gbs.notes: ap-southeast-7, first 6B GB-s/month tier ($0.0000135 next tier, $0.000012 over 15B). 400,000 GB-s/month free tier. Verified via AWS Price List API.

lambda_1m_inv.notes: ap-southeast-7: $0.00000018 per request (x86 and ARM same). First 1M requests/month free. Note this is cheaper than the common $0.20/1M in most regions.

apigw_1m.service: Amazon API Gateway REST API calls
apigw_1m.notes: ap-southeast-7 REST API, FIRST TIER (0-333M requests/month). Next tiers $3.177, $2.70, $1.719 per 1M. HTTP APIs are cheaper (not quoted here).

stream_hour.service: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams On-Demand stream

stream_gb_in.service: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams On-Demand data ingested
stream_gb_in.notes: ap-southeast-7 (APS9-OnDemand-BilledIncomingBytes). Data out $0.0441/GB standard consumer, EFO $0.0549/GB.

vdi_desktop_mo.service: Amazon WorkSpaces Personal, Standard bundle (2 vCPU/4GB, 80GB root/50GB user), Windows license included, AlwaysOn
vdi_desktop_mo.notes: REGION: ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) — WorkSpaces is NOT available in ap-southeast-7. Monthly (AlwaysOn) price; AutoStop alternative $14/mo + $0.42/hr. BYOL Windows variant $45/mo. Verified via AWS Price List API.

email_1k.notes: REGION: ap-southeast-1 — SES is NOT available in ap-southeast-7. $0.0001 per recipient/message (APS1-Recipients) x 1,000 = $0.10. Attachments billed $0.12/GB extra.

log_ingest_gb.service: Amazon CloudWatch Logs ingestion (Standard log class)
log_ingest_gb.notes: ap-southeast-7 (APS9-DataProcessing-Bytes). Storage/archival billed separately (~$0.03/GB-mo class dependent).

dns_1m.notes: First 1B queries/month tier; $0.20/1M above 1B. Route 53 authoritative DNS is a global service (queries priced the same regardless of region); hosted zone $0.50/zone-month extra. Verified via AWS Price List API.

queue_1m.notes: ap-southeast-7, Tier 1 (first 100B requests/month); first 1M requests/month free. FIFO $0.43/1M. Each 64KB chunk = 1 request.

opensearch_node_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 — requested r6g.large.search is NOT offered in Thailand; r7g.large.search (2vCPU/16GB, newer gen) is the nearest. For reference, r6g.large.search in ap-southeast-1 = $0.202/hr (verified). EBS storage billed separately.

dms_instance_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7 — dms.c5.large is NOT offered in Thailand; dms.c6i.large (2vCPU/4GB, direct successor) used. Multi-AZ $0.36/hr. Included storage 50GB gp2; GoldenGate-analogue CDC replication use case.

redshift_vcpu_hour.notes: ap-southeast-7: ra3.xlplus node-hour = $1.1727 (verified, APS9-Node:ra3.xlplus). ra3.xlplus has 4 vCPU / 32GB -> $1.1727 / 4 vCPU = $0.293175 per vCPU-hr. Redshift Managed Storage additionally $0.02349/GB-mo.

quicksight_user_mo.notes: Per-user price is region-independent (verified on official pricing page 2026-07-24). Author Pro (with Amazon Q) $40/user-mo; Reader $3/user-mo. Account capacity/SPICE billed separately ($0.38/GB-mo Enterprise SPICE).

gcp.label: Google Cloud
gcp.summary: YES — a Thailand region is GA with published prices. Google Cloud launched the Bangkok region (asia-southeast3) on 2026-01-21 and it appears as a selectable region with published on-demand list prices in the region pickers on cloud.google.com pricing pages (Compute Engine, Cloud Storage, Filestore, Cloud SQL, Cloud Run, Memorystore, Datastream, BigQuery, Load Balancing, VPC network, Cloud VPN). All region-scoped rates below are therefore quoted for asia-southeast3 unless the note says otherwise. Prices were read directly out of the embedded per-region price dataset that backs the official pri…

vcpu_hour.service: Compute Engine — N2D custom machine type, custom vCPU
vcpu_hour.notes: REGION EXCEPTION: from asia-southeast1 (Singapore). N2D (and N2) are not offered in asia-southeast3 — the Bangkok region price list covers 41 regions for N2D and Bangkok is not among them. Bangkok's nearest published general-purpose custom shape is N4: $0.034…

ram_gb_hour.service: Compute Engine — N2D custom machine type, custom memory
ram_gb_hour.notes: REGION EXCEPTION: from asia-southeast1 (Singapore), same reason as vcpu_hour. Bangkok N4 custom memory equivalent: $0.0039029 per GiB-hour (asia-southeast3). Singapore N2 custom memory for reference: $0.0054873/GiB-hr. Unit is GiB, not GB.

block_gb_mo.service: Compute Engine — Balanced Persistent Disk (pd-balanced) provisioned space
block_gb_mo.notes: asia-southeast3. Hourly equivalent on the same page: $0.000143836/GiB-hr. Regional (dual-zone) balanced is $0.21/GiB-month. Singapore comparison: $0.1099/GiB-mo (0.000150685/hr x 730).

os_std_gb_mo.service: Cloud Storage — Standard storage, single region
os_std_gb_mo.notes: asia-southeast3 single-region bucket. Singapore is $0.020. Cloud Storage bills in GiB, not GB.

os_ia_gb_mo.service: Cloud Storage — Nearline storage, single region
os_ia_gb_mo.notes: asia-southeast3. Nearline is GCP's infrequent-access class (30-day minimum storage duration). Coldline in Bangkok is $0.0055/GiB-mo if a colder tier is wanted.

os_arch_gb_mo.service: Cloud Storage — Archive storage, single region

os_retrieval_gb.service: Cloud Storage — Nearline data retrieval fee
os_retrieval_gb.notes: Global rate, no regional variance. Coldline $0.02/GiB, Archive $0.05/GiB. Standard and Rapid storage have no retrieval fee. This is on top of Class B operation and egress charges.

os_req_10k.service: Cloud Storage — Class B operations, Standard storage, flat namespace
os_req_10k.notes: Google publishes $0.0004 per 1,000 Class B ops for Standard storage in a single-region bucket (global rate, no regional variance). Math: $0.0004 x 10 = $0.004 per 10,000. Hierarchical-namespace buckets are $0.0005/1,000 -> $0.005/10,000. Nearline Class B is $…

fss_gb_mo.service: Filestore — Basic HDD tier (Standard), per-GiB capacity
fss_gb_mo.notes: asia-southeast3, Basic HDD tier (the tier Google labels 'Basic HDD (Standard)'). Hourly equivalent $0.000241096/GiB-hr. IMPORTANT: Basic HDD instances under 1 TiB also carry a per-instance fee of $36.30/month ($0.049726027/hr) which is waived at 1 TiB and abo…

alb_hour.service: Cloud Load Balancing — external Application Load Balancer forwarding rule
alb_hour.notes: Global rate (Google prices forwarding rules globally, not per region). $0.025/hr each for the first 5 forwarding rules, then $0.01/hr per additional rule. Monthly published equivalent $18.25 / $7.30. Covers global, classic and regional external Application Lo…

lb_data_gb.service: Cloud Load Balancing — data processed by load balancer
lb_data_gb.notes: asia-southeast3. Same $0.0084/GiB rate applies to both inbound and outbound processed data; charged per direction. Data processing is billed in the region where traffic is processed even for globally-scoped load balancers.

nlb_hour.service: Cloud Load Balancing — passthrough Network Load Balancer forwarding rule
nlb_hour.notes: Global rate. Internal and external passthrough Network Load Balancers use the same forwarding-rule pricing table as the ALB: $0.025/hr for the first 5 rules, $0.01/hr per additional rule. Passthrough NLBs incur no proxy-instance charge; regional data-processi…

fw_endpoint_hour.service: Cloud Next Generation Firewall (Cloud NGFW) Enterprise — firewall endpoint deployment
fw_endpoint_hour.notes: Global rate, no regional variance. Monthly published equivalent $1,277.50. The firewall endpoint is an organization-level resource billed to the billing project; one endpoint is required per zone you want L7 inspection in, so a 2-zone HA deployment is 2 x $1.…

fw_gb.service: Cloud NGFW Enterprise — data processing (traffic inspected by L7 security)
fw_gb.notes: Global rate. Charged for traffic in BOTH directions of a flow. Same $0.0193/GiB applies to the Cloud NGFW Standard tier (FQDN/threat-intel), but a flow matched by Enterprise rules is only charged once (the Standard charge is waived). Cloud NGFW Essentials and…

waf_base_mo.service: Google Cloud Armor Standard — security policy
waf_base_mo.notes: Global rate. $5.00 per policy per month ($0.006849315/hr) PLUS $1.00 per rule per month ($0.001369863/hr) — so a realistic WAF baseline is $5 + (n_rules x $1). Cloud Armor Enterprise Paygo is a different model at $200/month subscription covering 2 protected r…

waf_1m_req.service: Google Cloud Armor Standard — requests against a globally-scoped security policy
waf_1m_req.notes: Global rate. Regionally-scoped security policies (regional external ALB / internal ALB) are cheaper at $0.60 per 1M requests. Under Cloud Armor Enterprise, request charges are 'all included' in the subscription.

ic_1g_hour.notes: Product: Partner Interconnect (Google charges only for the VLAN attachment; the physical circuit is billed by the service provider separately and is not a Google list price). Global rate table, not region-specific. Bangkok-region attachments qualify for the d…

ic_10g_hour.notes: Product: Dedicated Interconnect. IMPORTANT for TCO: this $0.10/hr is the VLAN attachment only. The underlying Dedicated Interconnect connection (physical circuit) is charged separately at $2.328/hr per 10-Gbps circuit, so a realistic 10G Dedicated Interconnec…

vpn_tunnel_hour.service: Cloud VPN (HA VPN) — per tunnel attached to the gateway
vpn_tunnel_hour.notes: asia-southeast3. Monthly published equivalent $38.325 per tunnel. HA VPN requires 2 tunnels for the 99.99% SLA (4 against an AWS peer), so budget 2 x $0.0525/hr. Rate is identical for Classic VPN. IPsec traffic is billed separately at standard internet egress…

egress_gb.service: VPC — Premium Tier internet data transfer out (egress), first paid tier
egress_gb.notes: asia-southeast3, Premium Tier, 0–1,024 GiB/month band, destinations North America / Europe / Asia (excl. Korea and Indonesia). Next bands: $0.11 (1,024–10,240 GiB), $0.085 (>10,240 GiB). Destinations Australia/Indonesia/Korea/South America/Saudi Arabia are $0…

pg_vcpu_hour.service: Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL — Enterprise edition, dedicated-core vCPU
pg_vcpu_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Enterprise edition, zonal (non-HA). Monthly published equivalent $31.65645. HA (regional) doubles it to $0.08673/vCPU-hr. Google publishes one CPU/memory table covering both MySQL and PostgreSQL, so this rate is identical to mysql_vcpu_hour. …

pg_ram_gb_hour.service: Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL — Enterprise edition, memory
pg_ram_gb_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Enterprise edition, zonal. Monthly equivalent $5.3655/GiB. HA doubles to $0.0147/GiB-hr. Same table as MySQL. Enterprise Plus: $0.00945/GiB-hr.

pg_storage_gb_mo.notes: asia-southeast3, zonal SSD. HA/regional SSD is $0.357/GiB-mo. HDD is $0.0945/GiB-mo. Backups (used) $0.084/GiB-mo. Hyperdisk Balanced capacity option is $0.12075/GiB-mo + IOPS/throughput charges. Same storage table applies to MySQL and PostgreSQL.

mysql_vcpu_hour.service: Cloud SQL for MySQL — Enterprise edition, dedicated-core vCPU
mysql_vcpu_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Enterprise edition, zonal. Google publishes a single 'MySQL and PostgreSQL pricing' CPU/memory table, so MySQL and PostgreSQL Enterprise rates are identical by design — not an assumption on my part.

mysql_ram_gb_hour.service: Cloud SQL for MySQL — Enterprise edition, memory
mysql_ram_gb_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Enterprise edition, zonal. Identical to PostgreSQL Enterprise (shared pricing table). HA doubles to $0.0147/GiB-hr.

k8s_cluster_hour.service: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) — cluster management fee
k8s_cluster_hour.notes: Global flat fee, charged in 1-second increments, identical for Standard and Autopilot and for zonal/regional clusters. Node compute is billed separately at Compute Engine rates. A GKE free tier gives $74.40/month in credits per billing account (one free zonal…

redis_gb_hour.service: Memorystore for Redis — Basic tier, M1 capacity tier (1–4 GiB)
redis_gb_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Basic tier. Price is tiered DOWN with instance size: M1 (1–4 GiB) $0.05145, M2 (5–10 GiB) $0.02835, M3 (11–35 GiB) $0.02415, M4 (36–100 GiB) $0.01995, M5 (>100 GiB) $0.0168 per GiB-hour. Standard (HA) tier M1 is $0.0672/GiB-hr. Use the tier m…

functions_gbs.service: Cloud Run functions — request-based billing, memory + CPU folded at 1 vCPU per GiB
functions_gbs.notes: asia-southeast3, Cloud Run services with request-based billing (the model Cloud Run functions use). Published components (active time): memory $0.0000025 per GiB-second, CPU $0.000024 per vCPU-second. Math at 1 vCPU per GiB: 0.0000025 + (1 x 0.000024) = $0.00…

functions_1m_inv.notes: asia-southeast3. First 2 million requests per month are free. Instance-based-billing Cloud Run services have no per-request charge at all (they pay CPU/memory for the full instance lifetime instead: $0.000018/vCPU-s and $0.000002/GiB-s in Bangkok).

apigw_1m.service: API Gateway — API calls (billed via Service Control)
apigw_1m.notes: Global rate, no regional variance. Tiers: first 2M calls/month free, 2M–1B at $3.00 per million, above 1B at $1.50 per million. Egress from API Gateway is billed separately at Premium Tier rates ($0.12/GiB for the Asia-Pacific-to-Asia-Pacific first band).

pubsub_tib.notes: Explicitly '$40 per TiB in all Google Cloud regions' — no regional variance. First 10 GiB/month per billing account free. BigQuery and Cloud Storage subscriptions are $50/TiB; import topics $50–$80/TiB. Message storage is a separate $0.27/GiB-month.

log_ingest_gb.service: Cloud Logging — log ingestion into log buckets
log_ingest_gb.notes: Global rate. One-time charge for streaming logs into bucket storage; includes up to 30 days retention, indexing and querying at no extra cost. First 50 GiB per project per month free. Vended network logs (VPC Flow Logs, Firewall Rules Logging, Cloud NAT) are …

dns_1m.notes: Global rate, no free tier for Cloud DNS. $0.40 per million for the first 1 billion queries/month, $0.20 per million above. Routing-policy queries (geo/weighted/failover) cost more: $0.70 per million. Managed zones are billed separately at $0.20/zone/month for…

queue_1m.service: Cloud Tasks — billable operations (API calls and push delivery attempts)
queue_1m.notes: Global rate. First 1 million operations per month free; $0.40 per million up to 5 billion; above that contact sales. Operations are chunked at 32 KB — a 96 KB task counts as 3 operations. Egress for HTTP-target tasks is billed separately at network rates.

datastream_gb.service: Datastream — change data capture (CDC) data processed
datastream_gb.notes: asia-southeast3, first tier (0–2,500 GiB/month). Tiered down: $1.575 (2,500–5,000 GiB), $1.26 (5,000–10,000 GiB), $0.84 (>10,000 GiB). Backfill is separate and cheaper: first 500 GiB/month free then $0.42/GiB. AlloyDB and Spanner sources get 100 GiB/month of …

bq_slot_hour.service: BigQuery — Enterprise edition slot (pay-as-you-go capacity compute)
bq_slot_hour.notes: asia-southeast3, Enterprise edition, pay-as-you-go, billed per second with a 1-minute minimum. Monthly published equivalent $52.56 per slot. With commitments: $0.0576/slot-hr (1-year) and $0.0432/slot-hr (3-year). This is compute only — BigQuery storage and s…

gcve_node_hour.service: Google Cloud VMware Engine — ve1-standard-72 node (portable VCF license)
gcve_node_hour.notes: TWO CAVEATS, both material. (1) REGION: VMware Engine is not offered in asia-southeast3 — this rate is asia-southeast1 (Singapore); Sydney is identical. (2) BASIS: the current official pricing page no longer publishes an on-demand hourly rate for ve1-standard…

bi_user_mo.service: Looker Studio Pro (page currently branded 'Data Studio Pro') — Project subscription
bi_user_mo.notes: Product identified: Looker Studio Pro, the paid tier of Looker Studio (self-service Looker Studio remains free). Billed as '$9 per user per project per month' — note the per-PROJECT scoping, so a user in three projects is billed three times. Global price, not…

vcpu_hour.service: ECS General Computing S7n (s7n.large.2/.4, Linux, pay-per-use)
vcpu_hour.notes: Anchors read from official Price Calculator backend (region ap-southeast-2): s7n.large.2 (2 vCPU/4GB) = $0.0664/hr, s7n.large.4 (2 vCPU/8GB) = $0.0814/hr. Solve 2v+4r=0.0664, 2v+8r=0.0814 -> r=0.00375, v=(0.0664-0.015)/2=0.0257/vCPU-hr. s7n family IS sold in …

ram_gb_hour.service: ECS General Computing S7n (s7n.large.2/.4, Linux, pay-per-use)
ram_gb_hour.notes: r=(0.0814-0.0664)/4=0.00375/GB-hr from the two s7n anchor instances above.

block_gb_mo.notes: Native pay-per-use rate verified: $0.00015/GB-hour (Bangkok). Month=720h -> 0.00015*720=$0.108/GB-month. (High I/O $0.0000660/GB-hr, Ultra-high I/O $0.00027/GB-hr.)

os_std_gb_mo.notes: Hourly rate verified $0.0000305556/GB-hr = exactly $0.022/GB-mo at 720h. Multi-AZ (3AZ) = $0.025/GB-mo ($0.0000347222/GB-hr).

os_arch_gb_mo.notes: Verified $0.00000625/GB-hr = $0.0045/GB-mo (720h). Deep Archive also exists (cheaper, higher retrieval cost).

os_req_10k.notes: GET/read $0.004 ต่อ 10k (PUT/write $0.01) — เทียบฝั่ง read เหมือนค่ายอื่น

fss_gb_mo.notes: SFS Turbo Standard verified $0.000128/GB-hr -> *720 = $0.0922/GB-mo. Classic SFS capacity-oriented (SATA) = $0.000125/GB-hr = $0.09/GB-mo.

elb_hour.notes: Dedicated (v3) load balancer in Bangkok: $0.025/hr instance + LCU fee. Bangkok does NOT use shared-ELB-only pricing; legacy shared ELB (elbv2 s2.small) $0.053/hr also listed.

elb_lcu_hour.notes: $0.00833 per L4 or L7 LCU-hour (professional model). A basic per-AZ variant exists at $0/instance + $0.00695/LCU-hr/AZ.

fw_mo.notes: Standard is yearly/monthly only: $420/month. Professional: $1,450/month or pay-per-use $0.36/hour; EIP expansion $7/mo per extra EIP; VPC expansion (Pro) $300/mo.

waf_mo.notes: Cloud mode Standard edition (10 domains, ~2,000 QPS) $615/mo in Bangkok. Professional $1,560/mo, Platinum $4,730/mo. Pay-per-use cloud WAF also exists: $0.60 per million requests + $0.0014/rule-hour.

waf_extra.notes: Extra domain package $100/mo (Standard; $160 Professional, $320 Platinum). Rule expansion package $11/mo (all editions). Bandwidth expansion $160/mo (Standard).

dc_1g_mo.notes: AP-Bangkok port fee, monthly billing: 1GE $116/mo (Singapore is $138/mo). Carrier line + backhaul charged separately by the carrier.

vpn_gw_hour.notes: Pay-per-use: $0.33/hr for the gateway (first unit), + $0.035/hr per additional VPN connection beyond included quota. Professional2 (1 Gbps) $0.63/hr. Monthly option $209/mo (Prof.1).

egress_gb.notes: Bangkok tiered: $0.108/GB (0-10TB/mo), $0.077 (10-50TB), $0.074 (50-150TB), $0.072 (>150TB). EIP retention fee $0.005/hr applies while held.

pg_vcpu_hour.service: RDS for PostgreSQL n1 (general-enhanced), single node
pg_vcpu_hour.notes: Anchors verified (Bangkok, single, pay-per-use): rds.pg.n1.large.2 (2v/4GB)=$0.08/hr, rds.pg.n1.large.4 (2v/8GB)=$0.09/hr. r=(0.09-0.08)/4=0.0025; v=(0.08-0.01)/2=0.035. Primary/standby (HA) = 2x.

pg_ram_gb_hour.service: RDS for PostgreSQL n1, single node

pg_storage_gb_mo.notes: Verified $0.00027/GB-hr (single instance; doubled for HA) -> *720 = $0.1944/GB-mo. Ultra-high I/O legacy $0.0004/GB-hr; backup space beyond free quota $0.0002/GB-hr.

mysql_vcpu_hour.service: RDS for MySQL n1 (general-enhanced), single node
mysql_vcpu_hour.notes: rds.mysql.n1.large.2 (2v/4GB)=$0.08/hr and rds.mysql.n1.large.4 (2v/8GB)=$0.09/hr (identical to PG in Bangkok) -> v=$0.035/vCPU-hr, r=$0.0025/GB-hr.

mysql_ram_gb_hour.service: RDS for MySQL n1, single node

k8s_cluster_hour.service: CCE cluster management - cce.s1.small (non-HA, up to 50 nodes)
k8s_cluster_hour.notes: Bangkok: s1.small (single master, <=50 nodes) $0.13/hr; s1.medium (200) $0.26; HA s2.small $0.39, s2.medium $0.78, s2.large (1000) $1.50. Worker nodes billed as ECS.

functions_gbs.notes: AP-Singapore price used - Bangkok has no published FunctionGraph rate in the official calculator (nearest-region fallback). $0.00001667/GB-s after 400,000 free GB-s/month.

functions_1m_inv.notes: AP-Singapore fallback (Bangkok not published): $0.20 per million requests after first 1M free/month.

apig_hour.service: API Gateway - dedicated gateway, Basic edition
apig_hour.notes: Intl/Bangkok sells only DEDICATED gateways - no per-1M-call pricing exists. Basic (2,000 TPS) $0.76/hr; Professional (4,000) $3.46/hr; Platinum (10,000) $8.65/hr. Egress bandwidth extra.

kafka_hour.service: DMS for Kafka - smallest cluster (kafka cluster c3.mini, 100 MB/s)
kafka_hour.notes: Bangkok pay-per-use: c3.mini cluster $0.47/hr (small $0.84, middle $1.57, high $3.19). Disk storage billed separately (e.g. high-I/O for Kafka ~$0.01/hr per 100GB step).

vdi_desktop_mo.service: Workspace cloud desktop x86 Ultimate 2 vCPU/4GB (workspace.x86.ultimate.large2)
vdi_desktop_mo.notes: Ultimate series is the desktop family sold in AP-Bangkok; 2vCPU/4GB = $36/mo (or $0.089/hr pay-per-use). 2vCPU/8GB $57.2/mo. System/user disks billed separately ($0.092-$0.214/GB-mo).

log_ingest_gb.service: LTS (Log Tank Service) log read/write traffic
log_ingest_gb.notes: Bangkok: read/write (ingestion) $0.05/GB + index traffic $0.08/GB + storage $0.000125/GB-hr (~$0.09/GB-mo; cold storage ~$0.029/GB-mo). Budget ingestion+index = $0.13/GB for searchable logs.

dns_1m.notes: Free: DNS is not in the intl price calculator and Huawei promotes 'Free DNS' - public zone hosting and queries are currently not billed on the international site.

queue_1m.notes: AP-Singapore price (Bangkok publishes no request rate in calculator - nearest-region fallback): HTTP(S) $0.60/1M after 100k free/mo; API requests $0.50/1M after 1M free. Legacy DMS queue service is not sold intl; DMS RabbitMQ/Kafka are instance-based.

css_node_hour.notes: Bangkok: 4 vCPU/8GB computing-plus node $0.25/hr (4u16g $0.31, 8u32g $0.60). Node storage extra: ultra-high I/O $0.00027/GB-hr (~$0.19/GB-mo). No 2u8g flavor listed; 4u8g is the requested spec.

drs_hour.notes: Bangkok sync-task flavors: Micro $0.13/hr, Small $0.26, Medium $0.36 (typical default), Large $0.53, XLarge $0.84. First-time config period can be free (Charging Type: Config SKUs at $0).

dws_vcpu_hour.notes: Verified anchors (Bangkok, on-demand): dwsx2.2xlarge 8 vCPU/64GB = $0.5064/hr and dwsx2.4xlarge 16 vCPU = $1.0127/hr -> $0.0633/vCPU-hr (linear). Smallest node dwsx2.xlarge = $0.2532/hr. Node price includes bundled local storage.

waf_1m_req.notes: โหมด pay-per-use: $0.60/ล้าน request + $0.0014/rule-ชม. (edition รายเดือน Standard $615/เดือน 10 โดเมน)

azure.label: Microsoft Azure
azure.summary: All rates read from Microsoft's official Azure Retail Prices API (prices.azure.com, the data source behind the Azure Pricing Calculator) on 2026-07-24, region southeastasia, pay-as-you-go USD, plus official microsoft.com pages for Windows 365 and Power BI Pro. The announced Thailand region (Thailand Central) returns zero priced SKUs in the API, i.e. not GA with published prices, so Southeast Asia (Singapore) is used throughout. vCPU/RAM splits are derived by 2-equation solve from D2s_v5/E2s_v5 (VMs), Dadsv5/Eadsv5 2-vCore (PostgreSQL), and Ddsv5/Edsv5 per-vCore (MySQL). Application Gateway WA…

block_gb_mo.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Azure Premium SSD v2, Premium LRS Provisioned Capacity, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/managed-disks/
block_gb_mo.notes: API price $0.000132/GiB-hour x 730 h = $0.09636/GiB-mo. Includes 3,000 IOPS + 125 MB/s free baseline; extra IOPS/throughput billed separately.

os_std_gb_mo.service: Blob Storage - General Block Blob v2, Hot LRS
os_std_gb_mo.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (General Block Blob v2, Hot LRS Data Stored, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/storage/blobs/
os_std_gb_mo.notes: First 50 TB tier; drops to $0.0192 (50-500TB) and $0.0184 (>500TB).

os_ia_gb_mo.service: Blob Storage - General Block Blob v2, Cool LRS

os_arch_gb_mo.service: Blob Storage - General Block Blob v2, Archive LRS
os_arch_gb_mo.notes: 180-day early-delete applies; rehydration required before read.

os_retrieval_gb.notes: Cool tier retrieval. Archive standard retrieval $0.024/GB (priority $0.13/GB).

os_req_10k.notes: Hot tier reads; writes $0.05/10K; cool reads $0.01/10K.

fss_gb_mo.service: Azure Files (Files v2, Hot LRS, pay-as-you-go)
fss_gb_mo.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Files v2 Hot LRS Data Stored, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/storage/files/
fss_gb_mo.notes: At-rest data only; transactions billed separately. Legacy Standard transaction-optimized tier is $0.06/GB-mo with cheaper transactions.

alb_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Application Gateway WAF v2, Standard Fixed Cost, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/application-gateway/
alb_hour.notes: L7 LB with WAF built in - this same SKU is the WAF answer below. Standard_v2 without WAF is $0.26/hr.

lcu_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Application Gateway WAF v2, Standard Capacity Units, southeastasia)
lcu_hour.notes: 1 CU = 1 compute unit / 2,500 persistent connections / 2.22 Mbps. Standard_v2 CU is $0.008/hr.

nlb_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Load Balancer, Standard Included LB Rules and Outbound Rules); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/load-balancer/
nlb_hour.notes: Globally uniform meter (armRegion 'Global', applies to Southeast Asia). Plus $0.005/GB data processed and $0.01/rule-hr beyond 5 rules.

waf_base_mo.service: Application Gateway WAF_v2 fixed cost (monthly equivalent)
waf_base_mo.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Application Gateway WAF v2 Standard Fixed Cost, southeastasia)
waf_base_mo.notes: $0.468/hr x 730 h = $341.64/mo. Same SKU as alb_hour - Azure's L7 LB and WAF are one product; do not double-count when comparing.

dc_1g.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (ExpressRoute, Standard Metered Data 1 Gbps Circuit, Zone 2); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/expressroute/
dc_1g.notes: Monthly circuit fee (native unit is monthly, not hourly). Singapore peering location = Zone 2; same $436 in Zones 1-4. Metered plan adds ~$0.05/GB egress; Unlimited-data 1G is $8,700/mo Zone 2.

dc_10g.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (ExpressRoute, Standard Metered Data 10 Gbps Circuit, Zone 2)
dc_10g.notes: Monthly circuit fee, Zone 2 (Singapore). Egress billed separately on metered plan.

vpn_gw_hour.notes: 650 Mbps, up to 30 S2S tunnels; first 10 tunnels free then $0.015/tunnel-hr. Basic gateway is $0.036/hr (dev/test).

egress_gb.service: Bandwidth - internet data transfer out (Routing Preference: Internet)
egress_gb.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Bandwidth - Routing Preference: Internet, Standard Data Transfer Out, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/bandwidth/
egress_gb.notes: First paid tier 100 GB-10 TB/mo (first 100 GB/mo free). Falls to $0.075 then $0.07/$0.06 at higher tiers. Premium (Microsoft global network) routing is $0.12/GB first tier.

pg_vcpu_hour.service: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (Dadsv5/Eadsv5)
pg_vcpu_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (PostgreSQL Flexible Server GP Dadsv5 and MO Eadsv5 2 vCore, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/postgresql/flexible-server/
pg_vcpu_hour.notes: Anchors: GP D2ads_v5 (2 vCPU/8GB) $0.244/hr; MO E2ads_v5 (2 vCPU/16GB) $0.30/hr. 8r=0.056 -> r=$0.007; c=(0.244-0.056)/2=$0.094. Azure lists a bundled per-vCore price; this split is synthetic for comparison.

pg_ram_gb_hour.service: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

pg_storage_gb_mo.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Az DB for PostgreSQL Flexible Server Storage, Storage Data Stored, southeastasia)
pg_storage_gb_mo.notes: General-purpose storage; backup storage $0.095/GB-mo beyond free allotment.

mysql_vcpu_hour.service: Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server (Ddsv5/Edsv5)
mysql_vcpu_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (MySQL Flexible Server GP Ddsv5 vCore $0.1175/hr, MO Edsv5 vCore $0.1418/hr, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/mysql/flexible-server/

mysql_ram_gb_hour.service: Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server
mysql_ram_gb_hour.notes: r=(0.1418-0.1175)/4=$0.006075/GB-hr. MySQL Flexible storage is also $0.138/GB-mo.

k8s_cluster_hour.service: Azure Kubernetes Service - Free tier control plane
k8s_cluster_hour.notes: Free tier control plane costs $0 (no SLA, <=1,000 nodes). Standard tier (Uptime SLA) is $0.10/cluster-hr; Long Term Support $0.60/hr - both verified in API.

redis_gb_hour.service: Azure Cache for Redis Standard C1 (1 GB)
redis_gb_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Azure Redis Cache Standard C1 Cache $0.138/hr, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/cache/
redis_gb_hour.notes: C1 Standard (replicated 2-node, 1 GB) $0.138/hr -> $0.138/GB-hr. Per-GB falls with size: C2 (2.5GB) $0.225/hr=$0.09/GB-hr; C3 (6GB) $0.45/hr=$0.075/GB-hr. Basic (non-replicated) C1 is $0.055/hr.

functions_gbs.notes: First 400,000 GB-s/mo free (API tier min=400000). Newer Flex Consumption on-demand rate is $0.000037/GB-s with 100k GB-s free.

functions_1m_inv.notes: $0.000002 per 10 executions x 100,000 = $0.20/1M. First 1M executions/mo free.

apigw_1m.notes: $0.0425 per 10K x 100 = $4.25/1M. First 1M calls/subscription/mo free (API tier min=100 units of 10K).

tu_hour.notes: 1 TU = 1 MB/s or 1,000 events/s ingress, 2 MB/s egress. Plus ingress events $0.028/1M. Kafka endpoint included in Standard; Capture add-on $0.10/hr.

vdi_desktop_mo.notes: Flat per-user license, region-independent. Azure Virtual Desktop has no simple per-desktop list price (pay for underlying VMs/storage + M365 licensing), so Windows 365 is used as the comparable.

email_1k.service: Azure Communication Services - Email (Basic Sent Email)
email_1k.notes: $0.00025/email x 1000 = $0.25. Plus data transfer $0.00012/MB. Globally uniform meter.

log_ingest_gb.service: Azure Monitor Log Analytics - Analytics Logs ingestion (pay-as-you-go)
log_ingest_gb.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Log Analytics, Analytics Logs Data Ingestion, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/monitor/
log_ingest_gb.notes: First 5 GB/billing-month free (API tier min=5). Commitment tiers reduce effective rate; Basic Logs plan is cheaper per GB.

dns_1m.notes: First 1 billion queries/mo; $0.20/1M beyond. Plus $0.50/zone/mo (first 25 zones). Not free, unlike some providers.

queue_1m.notes: $0.004 per 10K x 100 = $0.40/1M (Class 1 and Class 2 same rate). Storage $0.045/GB-mo extra. Service Bus Standard alternative: $0.0135/hr base + $0.80/1M ops beyond 13M.

opensearch_node_hour.service: Azure AI Search - Standard S1 unit
opensearch_node_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Azure AI Search, Standard S1 Unit, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/search/
opensearch_node_hour.notes: Azure has no first-party managed Elasticsearch/OpenSearch (Elastic Cloud on Azure is Elastic-priced marketplace). Azure AI Search S1 (25 GB/partition) used as nearest first-party equivalent.

bi_user_mo.notes: Paid yearly; Premium Per User is $24/user/mo. Region-independent license.

vmware_node_hour.source: https://prices.azure.com/api/retail/prices (Specialized Compute Azure VMware Solution, AV36P VCF BYOL Node, southeastasia); https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/azure-vmware/
vmware_node_hour.notes: Post-Broadcom model: Southeast Asia lists only the VCF BYOL shape (bring your own Broadcom VCF license, license cost not included). No bundled-license AV36P SKU published for this region.

dw_vcpu_hour.notes: F2 = 2 CU = $0.44/ชม. → $0.22/CU-ชม.; เทียบ 1 CU ≈ 1 vCPU (โมเดล capacity ต่างจาก ECPU)

nt.label: NT Cloud (National Telecom)
nt.region: Thailand (primary site Nonthaburi, backup site Bangrak/Bangkok)
nt.summary: NT's marketing site (ntcloudsolutions.ntplc.co.th) publishes VM package specs (XS-XL) but no prices; the actual rate card is published in NT's official "NT Cloud Service (Prepaid)" user manual PDF (March 2024) via portal screenshots: VM sizes 1.60-22.10 THB/hour, SSD 0.006 THB/GB/hr, volume backup 0.003 THB/GB/hr, Public IP 0.15 THB/hr. Prices are pay-per-hour prepaid (top-up, min 500 THB); VAT treatment is not stated on the price screens (e-Tax invoice issued on top-up, so listed rates likely exclude 7% VAT). No public prices exist for object storage, load balancer, VPN, Kubernetes, managed …

vcpu_hour.notes: 2-anchor solve from official manual rate card: 2vCPU/4GB=1.60 and 2vCPU/8GB=2.80 THB/hr -> 2v+4r=1.60, 2v+8r=2.80 -> 4r=1.20 -> r=0.30, v=(1.60-1.20)/2=0.20. Cross-check exact: 4vCPU/8GB=4(0.20)+8(0.30)=3.20 (listed 3.20), 4vCPU/16GB=5.60 (listed 5.60). Large…

ram_gb_hour.notes: Same 2-equation solve as vcpu_hour (anchors 2vCPU/4GB=1.60, 2vCPU/8GB=2.80 THB/hr); r=0.30 THB per GB-RAM-hour. Verified against 4vCPU/8GB=3.20 and 4vCPU/16GB=5.60 exactly.

bundle_a.service: NT Cloud Service (Prepaid) — Cloud Server 2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM
bundle_a.notes: 2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, Ubuntu/Linux; disk NOT included (SSD 0.006 THB/GB/hr extra, OS needs min 10 GB), Public IP 0.15 THB/hr extra. Read from 'Choose Size' rate-card screenshot in NT's official prepaid manual (Mar 2024); live rates behind login at csp.ntplc.cloud…

bundle_b.service: NT Cloud Service (Prepaid) — Cloud Server 2 vCPU / 8 GB RAM
bundle_b.notes: 2 vCPU, 8 GB RAM (1:4 anchor). Full published ladder (THB/hr): 2c/4G=1.60, 4c/8G=3.20, 8c/16G=6.30, 16c/32G=12.50; 2c/8G=2.80, 4c/16G=5.60, 8c/32G=11.10, 16c/64G=22.10. Disk and Public IP billed separately. Approx 2,044 THB/mo at 730 h.

block_gb_mo.service: NT Cloud Service (Prepaid) — SSD Disk (block volume)
block_gb_mo.notes: Portal publishes 'SSD Disk Price: 1 GB = 0.006 THB/Hour' (verified in official manual screenshot). Monthly: 0.006 x 730 h = 4.38 THB/GB-month. Min 10 GB for OS disk.

backup_gb_mo.service: NT Cloud Service (Prepaid) — automatic volume backup
backup_gb_mo.notes: Portal publishes 'Volume backup will be charged at 1 GB 0.003 THB/Hour' (verified in manual screenshot). Monthly: 0.003 x 730 = 2.19 THB/GB-month. Note: postpaid Cloud Service includes free daily backup.

fw_mo.notes: Official NT Cloud page states 'ฟรี Firewall' — firewall is included free with every VM (postpaid), and security groups/firewall management are included in the prepaid self-service portal. Price 0 = genuinely free, not NO_EQUIVALENT.

egress_gb.notes: Official page states 'Data Transfer In-Out ฟรี!' and 'Data Transfer Unlimited' — egress is free; bandwidth is shared (20 Gbps domestic, 400 Mbps international).

nipa.label: Nipa.Cloud
nipa.region: NCP-BKK (Bangkok, Thailand; identical prices published for NCP-BKK2 Rama 9 and NCP-NON Nonthaburi)
nipa.summary: Prices read from the official nipa.cloud/pricing/nipa-space/* pages (JS-rendered Next.js pages; values confirmed from both the rendered tables and the pages' embedded pricing data). Compute, compute image, block storage, snapshots, external IP, load balancer, MySQL database instance, object storage, and internet egress are all published in THB for Thai AZs; Kubernetes, VPN, firewall appliance, and VDI have no published prices. Caveats: listed prices are stated as "based on an annual subscription for our self-service plan" (other terms are quote-only), monthly = hourly x 730, and NO VAT statem…

vcpu_hour.service: NCS Compute Instance — dedicated vCPU (All Purpose/Compute Intensive classes)
vcpu_hour.notes: 2-anchor solve from verified hourly flavors: apa.large.v2 (2 vCPU, 8 GB, 1:4) = 2.82 THB/hr and coa.large.v2 (2 vCPU, 4 GB, 1:2) = 2.33 THB/hr. 2v+8r=2.82; 2v+4r=2.33 -> 4r=0.49 -> r=0.1225; 2v=2.33-0.49=1.84 -> v=0.92. Model reproduces the whole dedicated li…

ram_gb_hour.notes: Same 2-equation solve as vcpu_hour (apa.large.v2 vs coa.large.v2): r=(2.82-2.33)/4=0.1225 THB/GB-hr (~89.4 THB/GB-mo at 730 h). Identical RAM rate falls out of the MySQL DB flavor solve, confirming linearity.

bundle_a.notes: 2 vCPU (shared), 4 GB RAM, 1/1 Gbps VPC bandwidth, SLA 99.0%. 1,030 THB/mo (1.41 THB/hr) at NCP-BKK. No disk included — add block storage. Monthly = hourly x 730; annual self-service package pricing.

bundle_b.service: NCS Compute Instance apa.large.v2 (All Purpose, dedicated)
bundle_b.notes: 2 vCPU (dedicated, AMD EPYC), 8 GB RAM. 2,060 THB/mo (2.82 THB/hr) at NCP-BKK. Lineup scales linearly up to apa.24xlarge.v2 (96 vCPU/384 GB, 98,880 THB/mo). No disk included. Same price in all three AZs.

block_gb_mo.notes: Standard-SSD 3 THB/GiB/mo (12 IOPS/GiB). Also published: Standard-HDD 1 THB/GiB/mo (2 IOPS/GiB), Premium-SSD 5 THB/GiB/mo (60 IOPS/GiB). Same in all AZs.

os_std_gb_mo.notes: Tiered: first 10 TB 0.77, next 90 TB 0.74, over 100 TB 0.70 THB/GB/mo. Requests extra: PUT/COPY/POST/LIST 0.15 THB per 1,000; GET/SELECT 0.013 THB per 1,000. Download (IIG) egress billed per bandwidth tiers; upload free.

backup_gb_mo.service: NCS Volume Snapshot — Standard-SSD (closest to backup)
backup_gb_mo.notes: No managed backup service is published; volume snapshot pricing used as proxy: Standard-HDD 0.5, Standard-SSD 1.5, Premium-SSD 2.5 THB/GiB/mo.

lb_mo.notes: 1,700 THB/mo (2.33 THB/hr), 49k network TPS / 18k HTTPS TPS. Cheaper Development tier lss.large.v2 = 1,030 THB/mo; HA topology lhd.large.v2 = 3,400 THB/mo (4.66 THB/hr). No per-connection or per-GB-processed charge published (new-connection fee listed as 0).

mysql_vcpu_hour.notes: 2-anchor solve from verified DB flavors: dpa.large.v1 (2 vCPU/8 GB, 1:4) = 2.82 THB/hr and dma.large.v1 (2 vCPU/16 GB, 1:8) = 3.80 THB/hr. 2v+8r=2.82; 2v+16r=3.80 -> 8r=0.98 -> r=0.1225; v=0.92. Reproduces dma.2xlarge.v1 (8/64): 8*0.92+64*0.1225=15.20~15.23 p…

egress_gb.notes: Domestic (in-Thailand) bandwidth is FREE. International egress tiers, recalculated monthly: first 1 TiB 2.31; next 49 TiB 2.18; next 100 TiB 1.77; next 350 TiB 1.63; over 500 TiB 1.09 THB/GiB. Upload free. Related: external IP 170 THB/mo (0.24 THB/hr); unused…

alibaba.label: Alibaba Cloud
alibaba.summary: Alibaba Cloud publishes Thailand (Bangkok) prices for most core services. ECS and disk prices were read from Alibaba's official international price JSON (g.alicdn.com ecs-price-info-intl v2.0.410, published 2026-07-23) which backs the ECS pricing-list page; OSS/NAS/CDT prices from Bangkok region tables embedded in official pricing pages; LB/firewall/WAF/VPN/Express Connect/ACK/FC/Kafka/SLS/SMQ/MaxCompute/Quick BI from official Documentation Center billing pages (some are global or region-group reference prices). RDS PG/MySQL pay-as-you-go hourly is NOT published on the intl website, so per-vC…

vcpu_hour.service: Elastic Compute Service (ECS) g7/r7 pay-as-you-go, Linux, Thailand (Bangkok)
vcpu_hour.notes: Anchors: ecs.g7.large 2v/8GB = $0.091314/hr and ecs.r7.large 2v/16GB = $0.115664/hr (Bangkok, PAYG effective price; pre-discount originals $0.1026/$0.12996). RAM = (0.115664-0.091314)/8 = 0.00304375/GB-hr; vCPU = (0.091314-8*0.00304375)/2 = 0.033482. Price fi…

ram_gb_hour.notes: See vcpu_hour: (r7.large - g7.large)/8 GB, Bangkok PAYG.

block_gb_mo.notes: Bangkok (ap-southeast-7): $15.20 per 100 GB per month ($0.02532/100GB-hr hourly PAYG). Same file version as ECS prices.

os_std_gb_mo.notes: Thailand (Bangkok) region table; first 5 GB free. ZRS = $0.018.

os_ia_gb_mo.notes: Thailand (Bangkok). 30-day minimum storage duration, 64KB minimum object size.

os_retrieval_gb.notes: Thailand (Bangkok). Archive retrieval $0.0095/GB ($0.030/GB for real-time access of Archive).

os_req_10k.notes: Thailand (Bangkok), Standard storage reads: first 500 million/month FREE, then $0.0014/10k. IA reads $0.010/10k; Standard writes free to 100M then $0.014/10k.

fss_gb_mo.notes: Thailand (Bangkok) region table. General-purpose Capacity NAS $0.09; Premium $0.13; Performance $0.32/GB-mo.

alb_hour.service: Application Load Balancer (ALB) Standard edition instance fee
alb_hour.notes: Doc reference price (region-uniform reference; buy page prevails). Basic $0.007, WAF-enabled $0.035. Doc updated 2026-06.

lcu_hour.notes: LCU = max(new conns/25 per s, concurrent conns/3000, GB processed, rule evals/1000). Billed per listener on highest dimension.

nlb_hour.notes: Doc reference price; NLB also bills LCU fee separately plus EIP data transfer.

fw_endpoint_hour.notes: $0.36 per firewall instance-hour (one Internet Firewall per protected region; one NAT FW per NAT GW; one VPC FW per TR/VPC pair). Native billing shape = instance-hour + traffic.

fw_gb.notes: Total traffic across Internet/NAT/VPC boundary firewalls; up to 10 Gbps peak. Subscription Premium/Enterprise editions also exist (monthly, quote on buy page).

waf_base_mo.notes: SeCU unit price $0.01; instance = 0.5 SeCU/hr = $0.005/hr x 730 h = $3.65/mo. Domain onboarding adds SeCU (e.g. 2nd-10th CNAME domain 5 SeCU/domain/hr).

waf_1m_req.notes: 1 SeCU per 5,000 requests/hr; 1M req = 200 SeCU x $0.01 = $2.00. Protection features (bot mgmt, API security) consume additional SeCUs.

dc_1g.service: Express Connect dedicated physical port, 1 Gbps
dc_1g.notes: Monthly subscription, regions outside Chinese mainland (Bangkok included). One-off port setup fee $1,500 waived when outbound data transfer billing enabled; 50 TB/mo free outbound quota until 2026-12-31.

dc_10g.service: Express Connect dedicated physical port, 10 Gbps

vpn_gw_hour.notes: $0.05/connection/hr in all regions except Dubai/Riyadh ($0.06); higher-bandwidth connection class $0.5/connection/hr. Outbound traffic billed via CDT.

egress_gb.service: Internet egress (Cloud Data Transfer, BGP), first paid tier
egress_gb.notes: Thailand (Bangkok): CDT tier 0-10TB $0.100/GB (10-50TB $0.070; 50-150TB $0.065; >150TB $0.055). 200 GB/month free for regions outside mainland China. Legacy ECS pay-by-traffic rate for Bangkok is $0.081/GB.

pg_vcpu_hour.service: ApsaraDB RDS for PostgreSQL (High-Availability), vCPU component
pg_vcpu_hour.notes: Bangkok, HA edition, general-purpose, official calculator 1-month subscription: 2c8g=$143.25, 4c8g=$219.75, 4c16g=$280.05 (incl. 20GB Premium ESSD $6.45). vCPU=(219.75-143.25)/2=$38.25/mo /730=$0.0524/hr. PAYG hourly is not published on the intl site and is t…

pg_ram_gb_hour.service: ApsaraDB RDS for PostgreSQL, RAM component

pg_storage_gb_mo.service: ApsaraDB RDS Premium ESSD storage (HA, both nodes)
pg_storage_gb_mo.notes: Calculator expense detail: 20 GB = $6.45/mo for HA edition in Bangkok => $0.3225/GB-mo (covers primary+standby replica storage).

mysql_vcpu_hour.service: ApsaraDB RDS for MySQL (High-Availability), vCPU component
mysql_vcpu_hour.notes: Bangkok, HA, dedicated class, 1-month subscription: 2c8g=$160.29, 2c16g=$188.82, 4c8g=$249.39 (incl. $6.45 storage). vCPU=(249.39-160.29)/2=$44.55/mo /730=$0.06103/hr. PAYG hourly not published on intl site.

mysql_ram_gb_hour.service: ApsaraDB RDS for MySQL, RAM component

k8s_cluster_hour.service: Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Pro - cluster management fee
k8s_cluster_hour.notes: USD 0.09 per cluster per hour pay-as-you-go (doc notes prices may vary by region). ACK Basic managed clusters are free (control plane); worker nodes billed as ECS.

redis_gb_hour.notes: Intl website does not publish Tair/Redis instance USD prices (buy page only). Estimate ~$0.05/GB-hr for Community edition 1-2GB HA master-replica in Singapore/Bangkok, from knowledge of console pricing. Verify on buy page.

functions_gbs.notes: FC bills unified CUs: tier-1 price $0.000020/CU; memory factor 0.15 CU/GB-s => $0.000003/GB-s. NOTE: vCPU billed separately at 1 CU/vCPU-s = $0.00002/vCPU-s (unlike AWS-style bundled GB-s). Monthly tiered per region; $0.01/hr minimum for active functions.

functions_1m_inv.notes: 0.0075 CU/invocation x 1M = 7,500 CU x $0.000020 = $0.15 per million.

apig_hour.notes: Native shape is now instance-hour (Cloud-native API Gateway): apigw.small.x1 $0.291/hr (doc example price, Chinese mainland; intl region price on buy page) + data processing $0.005/GB (Internet). Legacy shared API Gateway per-1M-call pricing (~$0.45/1M) is be…

kafka_hour.service: ApsaraMQ for Kafka - Standard Edition (High Write) alikafka.hw.2xlarge (3x20 MB/s)
kafka_hour.notes: Thailand (Bangkok) is Region group 3 = $0.420/hr for smallest standard spec; plus disk (ultra $0.01/100GB-hr, SSD $0.03/100GB-hr) and optional extra partitions. Professional hw.2xlarge = $1.06/hr in group 3. Serverless edition also exists.

vdi_desktop_mo.service: WUYING / Elastic Desktop Service (EDS) - 4vCPU/8GB office desktop
vdi_desktop_mo.notes: Intl docs show billing structure but not USD price tables (doc example cites ~$53.30/mo compute unit + $0.051/GiB storage; +80GB disk ~ $57/mo total). Region: Singapore reference; verify on EDS buy page.

email_1k.notes: $0.29 per 1,000 messages; 2,000 free emails per account (max 200/day). Resource plans cheaper (e.g. 1M messages $230.55).

log_ingest_gb.notes: $0.061/GB raw ingested (includes 30-day hot storage & features). Extended storage $0.002875/GB-day; global reference price (buy page prevails).

dns_1m.service: Alibaba Cloud DNS - Public Zone (authoritative), free edition
dns_1m.notes: Free edition: authoritative DNS hosting free with no per-query metering (QPS-capped, no SLA). Paid editions are per-domain monthly/yearly subscriptions, not per-query.

queue_1m.service: Simple Message Queue (formerly MNS) - standard queue requests
queue_1m.notes: $0.32 per million requests (doc example, China (Hangzhou); ~uniform). First 20M requests/month free.

opensearch_node_hour.service: Alibaba Cloud Elasticsearch - data node (2c8g class)
opensearch_node_hour.notes: Intl site publishes no USD node prices (buy page only). Estimate ~$0.22/hr for a 2-vCPU/8GB data node in Singapore from knowledge of console pricing; storage extra. Verify on ES buy page.

bi_user_mo.notes: Yearly subscription only: 5 users = $700/yr => 700/(5x12) = $11.67/user-mo (10 users = $10.83; volume tiers cheaper). Enterprise Standard 50 users = $18,000/yr = $30/user-mo. SaaS - not region-specific (intl sites: SG, HK, MY, ID, JP, US, DE).
